rule.desc: Use the most specific type possible. Prefer `Sequence[str]` over `list`, `Mapping[str, int]` over `dict`.
rule.rationale: Precise types communicate intent, enable better type checking, and document constraints.

example.code: # Good - specific and flexible def process_names(names: Sequence[str]) -> list[str]: """Process names, accepting any sequence type.""" return [name.upper() for name in names]

rule.tool_support: mypy detects type mismatches. Use `from collections.abc import Sequence, Mapping, Iterable`.
